meta.title: Emberfall
meta.subtitle: A roguelite about hoarding fire
meta.tagline: Every run you burn brighter — and shorter.
meta.author: Jane Dev

sections.heading: 1. Executive Summary
sections.body:
Emberfall is a top-down action roguelite where the player's health and their only weapon are the same resource: fire.

Spending flame to attack shortens the run; banking it makes you fragile. The tension of the core loop is that spend-vs-save decision, made hundreds of times per run.
bullets: Pitch: Hades meets a candle burning down.
USP: health and ammo are one shared meter.

subsections.heading: 1.1 Vision Statement
subsections.body: Make the player feel the weight of every attack by taxing the thing that keeps them alive.

sections.heading: 2. Core Mechanics
sections.body: The Flame Meter drives combat, movement, and death.

slides.title: The Hook
bullets: Spend to win the fight, save to survive the run.
slides.notes: Open on the shared-meter reveal; it lands in 5 seconds.

slides.title: Why Now
bullets: Roguelite audience is huge and loyal.
One-resource design reads instantly in trailers.

one_pager.title: EMBERFALL
one_pager.tagline: Every run you burn brighter — and shorter.
pillars: One resource for life and damage
Runs that visibly darken as you play
one_pager.usp: Health and ammunition are a single shared meter, making every attack a survival decision.
one_pager.target: Core action-roguelite players, 18-34, on PC and Switch.
one_pager.monetization: Premium, $19.99. Optional cosmetic ember skins post-launch.
